keys.description: Hard cap on delegation window (ends_at - starts_at). Enforced by valid_delegation_window rule + observer.
Minimum hours between now() and starts_at. Default 0 allows immediate delegation. delegation_type=emergency bypasses this check regardless of value.
Send DelegationActivatedNotification when the delegation becomes effective.
Send DelegationExpiredNotification when ends_at is reached.
How many hours before ends_at to fire DelegationExpiringNotification.
Min characters for the delegation reason (audited context field).
Default hard cap on session duration. Enforced by ImpersonationSessionObserver. Extended TTL requires impersonation_extended_ttl entitlement.
Cap for extended-TTL sessions. Still not unlimited — 4 hours is the negotiated upper bound.
Min characters for the impersonation reason (heavier than delegation's 20).
Global default. Can be flipped per-session by a super_admin if the tenant holds impersonation_extended_ttl entitlement.
When true, support_ticket_reference is required at session start. Some tenant contracts require this.
When true, is_read_only=true requires the target tenant to hold read_only_impersonation entitlement (enterprise). Setting false is a policy relaxation for testing only.
Enforces the one-at-a-time invariant per operator. Setting false is a policy relaxation for testing only.
Impersonation session evidence retention. SOC 2 Type II compliant.
Role delegation retention window before hard-purge (soft-deleted rows only).
After session end + this many days, session_token_hash is NULLED (row preserved). Hash residue removal.

The impersonated User is ALWAYS notified. This is a regulatory requirement (SOC 2 + PCI-DSS 8.5); no other value is supported. The key exists for future extensibility (e.g. a locale-specific mail-transport override).
DelegationResolver LRU cache TTL. Short by design — a newly-activated delegation should be honoured near-immediately in the next request.
How many top-request tenants to warm the resolver cache with on ApplicationBooted.
